content: Changed bare 'except Exception:' to 'except Exception as e:' in health/setup connection probe (unraid.py ~line 107). Added: (1) connection_error_type = type(e).__name__ capture, (2) logger.debug() with exception type and message, (3) status_note now includes the exception class name when connection fails (e.g. 'connection test failed (SSLCertVerificationError) — may be a transient outage or misconfiguration'). All 27 setup-related health tests pass. One pre-existing failure in test_check_api_error is in _health.py (not this bead's file) caused by unrelated uncommitted changes in the working tree.

Narrowed except Exception in _comprehensive_health_check to (httpx.ConnectError, httpx.TimeoutException, httpx.NetworkError). Added import httpx to _health.py. Updated test_check_api_error to use httpx.ConnectError (real connection failure → still returns {status: unhealthy}) and added test_check_api_error_logic_bug_propagates to assert AttributeError propagates as ToolError. 836 tests pass.

Wrong patch targets were unraid_mcp.subscriptions.utils.UNRAID_API_URL and unraid_mcp.subscriptions.manager.UNRAID_API_KEY and unraid_mcp.subscriptions.utils.UNRAID_VERIFY_SSL. Both utils.py and manager.py access settings via 'from ..config import settings as _settings', then read _settings.UNRAID_API_URL / _settings.UNRAID_API_KEY / _settings.UNRAID_VERIFY_SSL. Patching the module-local attribute name does nothing because those names don't exist in those modules. Correct patch targets are unraid_mcp.config.settings.UNRAID_API_URL, unraid_mcp.config.settings.UNRAID_API_KEY, and unraid_mcp.config.settings.UNRAID_VERIFY_SSL — the actual module-level variables that the settings module exports and all subscribers read through the _settings alias." 2>&1

Added logger.info at the top of the with tool_error_handler block in the flash_backup path (_disk.py:117-119). The log message includes subaction, remote_name (repr), and source_path (repr) — enriched over the plain subaction-only format used in non-destructive handlers, because flash_backup is destructive and the remote and source context are essential for audit traces. Placed immediately after entering the with block, consistent with all other disk handlers. 144 tests pass (test_storage.py + safety/)." 2>&1

Extensibility guard via _HANDLED_COLLECT_SUBACTIONS frozenset. When COLLECT_ACTIONS in subscriptions/queries.py gains a new key without a matching if-branch in _handle_live, the new test test_collect_actions_all_handled() fails with a clear message rather than silently hitting the runtime ToolError sentinel. Pattern: define a module-level frozenset of handled keys; assert COLLECT_ACTIONS.issubset(frozenset) in tests." 2>&1

Added null-response handling to simple_dict dispatch in _handle_system. (1) ups_device is now handled before the simple_dict loop — null upsDeviceById raises ToolError('UPS device X not found') with the device_id interpolated. (2) registration null raises ToolError('No registration data returned — server may be unlicensed or API unavailable') — license state is critical, silent {} was dangerous. (3) variables, metrics, config, owner, ups_config null logs logger.warning('system/X returned null from API') and returns {} — these are less critical and a degraded empty result is acceptable. flash was already handled via the simple_dict but also gets the warning path now. 8 new tests added to tests/test_info.py covering all null paths. 900/900 tests pass." 2>&1

All three test files use an identical pattern — module-level _mock_graphql fixture patches 'unraid_mcp.core.client.make_graphql_request' with AsyncMock, _make_tool() helper creates the tool via make_tool_fn(), and tests are grouped in classes with async methods (no @pytest.mark.asyncio decorator needed on class methods). sso_enabled returns {isSSOEnabled: bool}, vm/reboot uses field name 'reboot' (not camelCased) per _VM_MUTATION_FIELDS fallback, system/array ToolError is raised at line 164-165 when data.get('array') is falsy (None or missing key)." 2>&1

direct domain module imports eliminate fragile coupling through the unraid.py re-export shim. Previously, 119 schema tests imported from unraid_mcp.tools.unraid, meaning any removed re-export line (or a future decision to slim down the shim) would silently break all schema tests without touching any domain module. With direct imports, each test class depends only on the module it actually tests -- _system, _array, _disk, etc. -- so schema tests survive independent refactors of unraid.py. Also removed the unused module-level unraid_mod parameter from _all_domain_dicts() since all imports are now local to that function.

null-to-ToolError applied across three files. In _disk.py/logs: data.get('logFile') checked for None, raises ToolError with path in message. In _user.py/me: uses 'if not result' to catch both None and empty dict, raises ToolError with session-context message. In _oidc.py: provider checks for None and raises with provider_id in message; validate_session checks for None and raises with endpoint-unavailable message. Tests updated: test_storage.py and test_users.py had assertions expecting {} — both updated to expect ToolError. test_oidc.py had no null-response tests — three new tests added.

# Log LEARNED/PATTERN comments for all P1/P2 findings
Start errors collected in auto_start_all_subscriptions via TaskGroup inner function were never read or logged — silent startup failures. TaskGroup catches ExceptionGroup, not individual errors from _start_one. Pattern: always log or surface error lists after concurrent startup phases." 2>/dev/null

stop_subscription pre-existing bug: holds _task_lock while awaiting task cancellation. The new get_subscription_status fix (separate lock acquisitions) is correct but incomplete if stop_subscription is called concurrently — it can still deadlock via the held lock during await. Pattern: lock → snapshot → release → await is the safe pattern for asyncio cancellation." 2>/dev/null

Docstring written for the wrong reason — stated 'pong send is inside _data_lock' but no pong send exists there. The actual reason for splitting lock acquisitions is to avoid simultaneous hold of _task_lock+_data_lock which contends with _subscription_loop (which holds _data_lock across await points). Always verify docstring claims against actual code before committing." 2>/dev/null

_task_lock is a coarse lifecycle lock — using it for every live resource read serializes all concurrent reads (11 subscriptions × polling frequency). Pattern: use a dedicated read-oriented lock or snapshot method that avoids holding the lifecycle lock during I/O-adjacent reads. The existing _data_lock is already designed for read-heavy subscription data paths." 2>/dev/null

resources.py accessed subscription_manager._task_lock (private attribute) for correctness — but this breaks encapsulation. The safer design: add a public method to SubscriptionManager that returns just the data the caller needs, keeping the locking strategy internal. Pattern: never access _ prefixed attributes from external modules; add a public accessor instead." 2>/dev/null

repr() and json.dumps() produce different string lengths for complex objects. Using repr() to measure 'size' of a dict/list value that will be JSON-seria

content:
Default transport changed stdio → streamable-http. Rationale: aligns with primary deployment target (Docker/plugin); stdio remains available via UNRAID_MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio override." && \
Auth enforcement at ASGI/HTTP level (Starlette middleware wrapping FastMCP app), not MCP protocol level. Rationale: transport-level auth should intercept before framework sees the request; returns proper 401 HTTP responses." && \
Token auto-generated with secrets.token_urlsafe(32), written to ~/.unraid-mcp/.env, printed once on first generation only. Rationale: avoids token appearing in logs on every restart (mild security concern)." && \
UNRAID_MCP_DISABLE_HTTP_AUTH=true skips all auth enforcement. Target: gateway/proxy users (nginx, Caddy, Authelia, Authentik) who handle auth externally." && \
Permissions auto-fix (chmod 600 .env, 700 dir) rather than warn-only or refuse-to-start. Rationale: non-disruptive to advanced users, ensures security for common case." && \
FACT: Existing streamable-http transport already works (server.py:135-157). This work adds auth on top — no transport plumbing changes needed." && \
FACT: Existing middleware chain fires at MCP protocol level (tools/call, resources/read). BearerAuthMiddleware must be wired separately at ASGI level in server.py HTTP startup path.

content: FastMCP 3.x exposes mcp.http_app() as a clean public method returning the ASGI app. Phase 2 wraps it: app = mcp.http_app(path='/mcp'), then Middleware(app, token=settings.BEARER_TOKEN). No private internals needed. Also has run_http_async() if we want to delegate uvicorn startup to FastMCP.
Scope mode: HOLD — 3 phases, ~7 files, 1 new class. No scope changes. Deferred: token rotation, OAuth2/OIDC, multi-token management.

# Phase 1 (settings/token lifecycle) findings
FACT: settings.py bool env var pattern — raw=os.getenv('VAR','false').lower(); flag = raw in ('true','1','yes'). Use this exact pattern for UNRAID_MCP_DISABLE_HTTP_AUTH. Type annotation: bool (not bool|str — no third case here)." && \
FACT: _write_env in core/setup.py (lines 127-166) already handles ~/.unraid-mcp/.env write with line-replacement + chmod 600/700 pattern. Token write should follow this exact implementation — read lines, replace matching key, append if not found, write_text(), chmod(0o600)." && \
FACT: apply_runtime_config pattern exists in settings.py (lines 120-130) — mutates module global AND os.environ simultaneously. If token is auto-generated at startup, need a parallel function so settings reflect the new value without a process restart." && \
SECURITY: Print auto-generated token to STDERR not stdout. In stdio transport mode, stdout is piped directly to the MCP client. Printing to stdout would corrupt the MCP protocol stream. Use logger.info() (RichHandler writes to stderr) or explicit print(..., file=sys.stderr)." && \
SECURITY: Startup guard must NOT suggest DISABLE_HTTP_AUTH as the fix. Error message must be action-oriented: tell user to set UNRAID_MCP_BEARER_TOKEN or re-run the server to auto-generate. Use sys.exit(1) consistent with _parse_port pattern in settings.py." && \
dotenv.set_key(path, key, value, quote_mode='auto') is the correct tool for updating a single key in .env without clobbering other vars. Already a project dependency. Creates file with 0600 if absent, does targeted in-place edit preserving comments. Must mkdir dir first if not exists.
echo "Phase 1 comments done

content:
# Phase 2 (BearerAuthMiddleware) findings
SECURITY: hmac.compare_digest(provided, expected) is MANDATORY for constant-time token comparison. Standard == short-circuits and leaks token prefix via timing. Both args must be same type (str). This must be a locked decision." && \
SECURITY: RFC 6750 requires DIFFERENT WWW-Authenticate headers for missing vs invalid token. Missing: 'Bearer realm=\"unraid-mcp\"'. Invalid: 'Bearer realm=\"unraid-mcp\", error=\"invalid_token\", error_description=\"...\"'. Body: {\"error\":\"invalid_token\",\"error_description\":\"...\"}." && \
SECURITY: Bearer token value must NEVER appear in logs, error responses, or exception context. Add 'bearer' to _SENSITIVE_KEYS frozenset in core/client.py (defense-in-depth; 'token' already there but explicit is better). Never echo the provided token in a 401 response body." && \
SECURITY: DISABLE_HTTP_AUTH=true startup warning must be loud — multi-line boxed warning to stderr on every HTTP startup, not just logger.warning(). Wording: 'HTTP authentication is DISABLED. Any client on the network can control your Unraid server. Only use behind an authenticating reverse proxy.'" && \
FACT: FastMCP's http_app() accepts middleware: list[ASGIMiddleware] parameter directly (transport.py:279). run_http_async() also accepts middleware parameter and passes it to http_app(). This means we can inject middleware via mcp.http_app(middleware=[...]) or run_http_async(middleware=[...]) WITHOUT abandoning mcp.run() lifecycle management." && \
FACT: FastMCP 3.x ships a full native auth provider framework — FastMCP(auth=TokenVerifier) on the constructor. mcp.server.auth.middleware.bearer_auth.RequireAuthMiddleware already implements RFC 6750 correctly. However: FastMCP() is instantiated at module scope (server.py:76), not inside run_server(), complicating conditional auth injection." && \
PATTERN: BaseHTTPMiddleware subclass pattern for the middleware: class BearerAuthMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware): __init__(app, token, disabled=False); async dispatch(request, call_n

content:
# Phase 3 (docs/tests) findings + epic-level architectural conflict
FACT: Test patching rule from CLAUDE.md (updated 2026-03-28): patch make_graphql_request at unraid_mcp.core.client (not tool module level). Schema tests import from domain modules directly." && \
FACT: UNRAID_MCP_DISABLE_HTTP_AUTH must NOT appear in .env.example. Only document in README/CLAUDE.md. Presence in .env.example encourages users to uncomment it during debugging and forget to re-comment." && \
PATTERN: Middleware tests should use Starlette's TestClient or httpx.AsyncClient against the ASGI app directly. No need for a running uvicorn process for unit tests of the middleware." && \
Research complete — 6 domain-matched agents. Key findings: (1) hmac.compare_digest is mandatory; (2) RFC 6750 requires differentiated WWW-Authenticate headers; (3) http_app(middleware=[...]) accepts middleware directly — no need to bypass mcp.run(); (4) FastMCP has native auth= constructor param; (5) simplicity reviewer recommends removing auto-generation as YAGNI; (6) print token to stderr not stdout." && \
ARCHITECTURAL CONFLICT — Two approaches surfaced: (A) Custom BaseHTTPMiddleware passed via http_app(middleware=[...]) — matches locked brainstorm decision, straightforward, avoids framework internals. (B) FastMCP native auth= TokenVerifier subclass — framework-native but FastMCP() is at module scope complicating conditional injection, and imports authlib/cryptography. Approach A is simpler and sufficient." && \
SCOPE CONFLICT — Simplicity reviewer recommends removing auto-generation (YAGNI) and replacing with hard startup guard only. This conflicts with locked brainstorm decision to auto-generate + persist token. Needs user judgment before plan is locked.
echo "Epic comments done

content: Middleware approach — chose BaseHTTPMiddleware via http_app(middleware=[...]) over FastMCP native auth= constructor param. Rationale: FastMCP() is at module scope (server.py:76), complicating conditional injection; native auth pulls in authlib/cryptography complexity. http_app(middleware=[...]) is simpler, sufficient, and avoids fighting module-init ordering.

Auto-generation confirmed — keep locked brainstorm decision. Simplicity reviewer flagged as YAGNI; user explicitly chose to keep it. Rationale: UX convenience outweighs complexity cost for homelab use case. Token guard + auto-gen both remain in Phase 1.

Starlette request.headers is case-insensitive by key (get('Authorization') == get('authorization')). BUT the value is returned verbatim. RFC 7235 says auth-scheme is case-insensitive. Implementation MUST use auth.lower().startswith('bearer ') for prefix check, then extract token from the original value: token = auth[len('bearer '):] where auth is the original header value. Never compare auth.startswith('Bearer ') — would reject RFC-compliant clients sending lowercase 'bearer'.

CEO review mode: HOLD SCOPE — scope is right, make it bulletproof. 7 files, 1 new class, well-bounded." && \
Token timing — ensure_token_exists() called in run_server() before guard check, then apply_runtime_config(settings, token) mutates module globals + os.environ so guard and middleware see the live value. No main.py changes needed." && \
Default transport breaking change accepted. Document in CHANGELOG/README with migration note: set UNRAID_MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio to restore prior behavior. Minor version bump required. Plugin users unaffected (plugin.json hardcodes stdio)." && \
Auth failures log at WARNING level with client IP: logger.warning('401 Unauthorized: missing/invalid Bearer token from %s', client_ip). client_ip from request.client.host." && \
FACT: Empty string UNRAID_MCP_BEARER_TOKEN='' must be treated as None. Use: token = os.getenv('UNRAID_MCP_BEARER_TOKEN') or None. Empty string triggers auto-generation same as missing key." && \
FACT: apply_runtime_config must set BOTH the settings module global AND os.environ: settings.UNRAID_MCP_BEARER_TOKEN = token; os.environ['UNRAID_MCP_BEARER_TOKEN'] = token. Both are needed for in-process consistency." && \
FACT: Default transport change is a breaking change for direct uv run users. Phase 3 must add CHANGELOG entry and README migration note. Minor version bump (1.1.5 → 1.2.0) required. Also add test: empty BEARER_TOKEN string → auto-generates.

content:
DECISION: Middleware type changed from BaseHTTPMiddleware to pure ASGI pattern (async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send)). Rationale: 53% overhead reduction, no body buffering, WebSocket-safe. Locked decision updated." && \
DECISION: Per-IP rate limiting added to Phase 2 scope. Token bucket keyed on sanitized client IP, 60 failed auth attempts/min → 429 Too Many Requests. Addresses unauthenticated DoS vector (existing rate limiter is MCP-protocol level, after auth)." && \
DECISION: Phase 1 and Phase 2 must ship together as a single PR. Guard without middleware is a broken intermediate state." && \
Engineering review: pure ASGI middleware eliminates 53% overhead vs BaseHTTPMiddleware (0.187ms vs 0.286ms); pre-encode token bytes at __init__; pre-serialize 401 body as static bytes; per-IP rate limiting required for DoS hardening; sanitize client.host before logging.
echo "Done

FACT: Atomic token write — use os.open(path, os.O_CREAT|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, 0o600) + write + os.rename from temp file pattern, OR use dotenv.set_key() followed by immediate chmod 600. The TOCTOU window (write then chmod) is negligible for single-user homelab but atomic write is best practice. At minimum: mkdir -p the credentials dir before calling set_key()." && \
FACT: Bearer auth middleware tests go in tests/test_bearer_middleware.py (NOT tests/http_layer/) — http_layer/ tests the outbound GraphQL client (httpx/respx); inbound Starlette middleware is a different layer. Rename from the plan's http_layer/test_bearer_auth.py." && \
FACT: Add auth_enabled field to health/check output — makes DISABLE_HTTP_AUTH misconfiguration observable without log access. Health check already returns system state; adding auth_enabled: bool is a one-liner." && \
eng-review label: engineering review applied. 4 agents. Critical changes: pure ASGI middleware (locked), per-IP rate limiting (added to Phase 2), sanitize client.host (added), rate-throttle logging (added), atomic token write (added to Phase 1), auth_enabled in health check (added to Phase 3).
echo "Done

After UNRAID_MCP_BEARER_TOKEN is assigned to module variable, call os.environ.pop('UNRAID_MCP_BEARER_TOKEN', None) to clear it from the C environ array. Reduces secret inheritance by any child process or native library. One-line addition to settings.py after token assignment.
